title: chore(commons): H-FIX-ROOT-CAUSE м—җ 'лҢҖмІҙ' мҡ°нҡҢ мӢңк·ёл„җ м¶”к°Җ
body: - config/enforcement.json prompt_hint H-FIX-ROOT-CAUSE м—җ 'лҢҖмІҙ(?!лЎң)' м¶”к°Җ(18вҶ’19) вҖ” нғҖ-м–ём–ҙ/лҸ„кө¬лЎң к°Ҳм•„м№ҳмҡ°лҠ” 'лҢҖмІҙ' мҡ°нҡҢ к°җм§Җ, 'лҢҖмІҙлЎң'(мқјл°ҳм ҒмңјлЎң) мҳӨнғҗ м ңмҷё. #253 нӣ„мҶҚ.

title: feat(commons): fix-the-tool к·ңм№ҷ + prompt_hint к°•нҷ” вҖ” м–ём–ҙ/лҸ„кө¬ лІҪмқҖ мҡ°нҡҢ л§җкі  upstream-fix
body: - commons мӢ к·ң к·ңм№ҷ fix-the-tool вҖ” м–ём–ҙ/лҸ„кө¬ лІҪ(мҳҲм•Ҫм–ҙВ·нҢҢм„ңк°ӯВ·i64н•ңкі„В·stdlibл¶Җмһ¬В·codegenВ·л§ҒнҒ¬мӢӨнҢЁ)мқҖ мҡ°нҡҢ л§җкі  к·ё лҸ„кө¬лҘј м§Ғм ‘ upstream-fix(м „л¶Җ dancinlab repo). dont=нғҖ-м–ём–ҙ scratch мһ¬кө¬нҳ„В·мәҗмӢңл°”мқҙл„ҲлҰ¬В·мӢ¬ліјнҡҢн”јВ·fallbackВ·мёЎм •мў…м°© мЎ°кё°м„ м–ёмңјлЎң лҚ®кё°(=wire-to-prod dead). prompt_hint H-FIX-ROOT-CAUSE нҢЁн„ҙ 8вҶ’18 ліҙк°•+нһҢнҠё лӘ…л¬ёнҷ”лЎң л§Өн„ҙ prompt-scan н‘ңл©ҙнҷ”(кё°кі„к°•м ң). лҸҷкё°=PCF м„ём…ҳ i64/мҳҲм•Ҫм–ҙ лІҪ мҡ°нҡҢ л°ҳліө мӮ¬мҡ©мһҗм§Җм Ғ.

title: feat(guard): GIT-BRANCH-SWITCH-MAIN вҖ” л©”мқё мӣҢнҒ¬нҠёлҰ¬ лёҢлһңм№ҳ м „нҷҳ м°ЁлӢЁ(#3559 мһ¬л°ң л°©м§Җ) В· л§ҒнҒ¬л“ң мӣҢнҒ¬нҠёлҰ¬В·нҢҢмқјліөмӣҗ л©ҙм ң
body:
- мӢ к·ң мҪ”л“ңл ҲлІЁ к°Җл“ң GIT-BRANCH-SWITCH-MAIN(modules/git-checkout-guard.ts): MAIN мӣҢнҒ¬нҠёлҰ¬м—җм„ң HEAD мқҙлҸҷн•ҳлҠ” `git checkout <branch>`/`-b`/`-B`/`--orphan`/`--detach`/`-` л°Ҹ лӘЁл“  `git switch` м°ЁлӢЁ вҖ” кіөмң  primary мІҙнҒ¬м•„мӣғмқ„ к°Ҳм•„м—Һмңјл©ҙ лі‘л ¬ м„ём…ҳмқҳ untracked мһ‘м—…мқҙ лӮ м•„к°Җкі  мқҙнӣ„ м»Өл°Ӣмқҙ м—үлҡұ лёҢлһңм№ҳм—җ м•Ҳм°©(#3559 parallel-worktree мӮ¬кі  нҒҙлһҳмҠӨ).
- MAIN н•ңм •: git-dir м—җ `/worktrees/` м—ҶмқҢмңјлЎң нҢҗлі„ вҶ’ л§ҒнҒ¬л“ң/мһ„мӢң мӣҢнҒ¬нҠёлҰ¬лҠ” л©ҙм ң(лёҢлһңм№ҳ м „нҷҳмқҙ кІ©лҰ¬ мӣҢнҒ¬нҠёлҰ¬мқҳ ліё лӘ©м Ғ). л№„-repo cwd лҸ„ л©ҙм ң.
- нҢҢмқјліөмӣҗмқҖ нҶөкіј: `checkout -- <path>`, 2+ positional(`<tree-ish> <path>`)мқҖ HEAD лҜёмқҙлҸҷмқҙлқј л¬ҙл°ңнҷ”. лӘЁнҳён•ң lone `checkout <ref>` л§Ң `git rev-parse --verify <ref>^{commit}` мңјлЎң мӢӨм ң ref нҷ•мқё нӣ„м—җл§Ң м°ЁлӢЁ(`checkout README.md` к°ҷмқҖ нҢҢмқјліөмӣҗмқҖ rev мӢӨнҢЁ вҶ’ нҶөкіј).
- мқёлқјмқё override м—ҶмқҢ(force-push к°Җл“ңмҷҖ лҸҷмқј вҖ” кіөмң мғҒнғң ліҙнҳё) В· config `git.guardBranchSwitch` нҶ кёҖ кё°ліё ON. нӣ„ліҙ нҢҢмӢұлҗҗмқ„ л•Ңл§Ң git нҳём¶ң(per-bash latency 0).
- QA: нҢҢм„ң лӢЁмң„ 18/18 PASS В· e2e(мӢӨм ң cd) MAIN checkout-branch/-b/switch=BLOCK В· MAIN file-restore/statusВ·LINKED-wtВ·non-repo=ALLOW В· tsc clean.

title: refactor architecture
body: ARCHITECTURE нҠёлҰ¬ л…ёл“ң мҠӨнӮӨл§ҲлҘј мҳҒм–ҙ canonical нӮӨлЎң нҶөмқј вҖ” slugвҶ’id + н•ңкёҖнӮӨ мқҙлҰ„/м—ӯн• /мғҒм„ёвҶ’name/role/detail (models/datasets/convergence к°Җ мқҙлҜё id нӮӨ вҶ’ м •н•©). кІҢмқҙнҠё ARCH-SLUG-MISSING/DUPE/FORMATвҶ’ARCH-ID-* (severity-map Г—2). л Ҳкұ°мӢң нӮӨ(slugВ·мқҙлҰ„В·м—ӯн• В·мғҒм„ё)лҠ” read-fallback(nodeKey/nodeName/nodeRole/nodeDetail)лЎң мҡ©мқё вҖ” л§Ҳмқҙк·ёл Ҳмқҙм…ҳ мӨ‘В·нғҖ repo нҠёлҰ¬к°Җ к№ңк№ңн•ҙм§Җм§Җ м•ҠкІҢ. architecture.ts(нғҖмһ…В·skeletonВ·searchВ·lint)В·init.ts мҠӨмәҗнҸҙл“ңВ·ARCHITECTURE.html fallbackВ·cli HELPВ·READMEВ·мһҗкё° ARCHITECTURE.json м „ л…ёл“ң м „нҷҳ. кІҖмҰқ: id-dupe block к°•м ңВ·л Ҳкұ°мӢң н•ңкёҖл…ёл“ң read+search OKВ·architecture lint ok. L0 cli/index.ts HELP 1мӨ„ мқҳлҸ„м Ғ нҺём§‘(# no-verify-ok)

title: fix(self-update): non-destructive updateClone вҖ” stop wiping unmerged local commits on reset
body: self-update updateClone мқ„ NON-DESTRUCTIVE лЎң вҖ” blind reset --hard origin/main мқҙ нҒҙлЎ мқҳ лҜёлЁём§Җ лЎңм»¬ м»Өл°Ӣмқ„ л§ӨлІҲ мҶҢл©ёмӢңнӮӨлҚҳ root-cause м°ЁлӢЁ. fetch нӣ„ dirty лҳҗлҠ” origin/main ліҙлӢӨ ahead л©ҙ reset кұ°л¶Җ(skipped) вҶ’ selfUpdate к°Җ clobber лҢҖмӢ  warn(лЎңм»¬ мһ‘м—… ліҙнҳё В· main лЁём§Җ/push нӣ„ мһ¬мӢӨн–ү). лЁём§ҖлЎң ahead=0 лҗҳл©ҙ мһ¬к°ң. model.ts add() мқҳ dead k===sha ternary м ңкұ° + setup.ts res нғҖмһ…м—җ skipped? м¶”к°Җ вҶ’ tsc clean. origin/main лЁём§Җ(reset no-op)мҷҖ мқҙмӨ‘ л°©м–ҙ.

body: sidecar pod {poll|watch|unwatch|list} м¶”к°Җ вҖ” GPU pod мһҗлҸҷ нҸҙл§Ғ(>=10л¶„ cadence)мқ„ canonical лӘ…л №мңјлЎң мқјл°ҳнҷ”. лӘЁл“  мӣҗкІ© opмқҖ hexa cloud кІҪмң (alive/exec/copy-from/rm В· м§Ғм ‘ ssh/curl кёҲм§Җ). poll=one-shot read-only(teardown/pullмқҖ лӘ…мӢң н”Ңлһҳк·ёл§Ң В· pull-then-destroy a_fire_recover_complete). watch=л Ҳм§ҖмҠӨнҠёлҰ¬(~/.sidecar/pod-watch.json)+cadence: --cron(мӢңмҠӨн…ң crontab) лҳҗлҠ” agent ScheduleWakeup fallback(кё°ліё л№„нҢҢкҙҙ, macOS crontab write к¶Ңн•ң м—Ҷмқ„ л•Ң мһҗлҸҷ fallback). modules/pod-poll.ts мӢ к·ң + runbooks.ts runPod л””мҠӨнҢЁм№ҳ + index.ts HELP + commands/pod.md + TOOLKIT.jsonl лҸҷкё°.

ARCHITECTURE/README dataset л…ёл“ңм—җ mirror лӘ…мӢң.

title: fix(errors): severity-map лЎңл“ңлҘј bundled defaults + repo override лі‘н•©мңјлЎң вҖ” мғҲ bundled к·ңм№ҷмқҙ stale per-repo л§өм—җм„ң fallback-block л‘”к°‘н•ҳлҚҳ кІ°н•Ё м°ЁлӢЁ (warn к·ңм№ҷмқҙ м–ҙл””м„ңлӮҳ warn)
fix(architecture): ARCH-SCHEMA-UNRECOGNIZED вҖ” slug кІҢмқҙнҠё silent no-op н‘ңл©ҙнҷ” (мқҙлҰ„-л…ёл“ң 0 = off-schema м„Өкі„л¬ём„ң)
feat(pool): live RAM occupancy in 'pool list' вҡЎbadge (CPUВ·RAMВ·GPU)
body: pool list's live вҡЎbadge now includes system RAM occupancy alongside CPU and GPU. LOAD_PROBE gained a POSIX-sh RAM segment: Linux reads /proc/meminfo (used = MemTotal вҲ’ MemAvailable), macOS falls back to vm_stat free+inactive Г— hw.pagesize vs hw.memsize. probeLoad parses RAM=<usedMiB,totalMiB>; fmtLoad renders 'RAM <pct>%В·<used>/<total>G' between the CPU and GPU segments. status/specs still show static capacity only вҖ” live occupancy is list-only.

title: feat(changelog): auto-prune to keep-N (add-time + SessionStart autoprune) + fix(pr-cycle): explicit --head + worktree-aware base sync
body: Two follow-ups surfaced by the cross-repo CHANGELOG sweep. (A) changelog auto-prune: 'add' now auto-trims the working file to keep-N (config lint.changelog.keep, default 30) after appending, and a new 'autoprune' verb (SessionStart-wired via setup.ts) trims to keep-N when over cap (silent no-op under cap); full history stays in git. (B) pr-cycle fixes: gh pr create now passes explicit --head <branch> (worktree head-inference quirk), and the post-merge local-base ff-sync now reports a benign skip (not a misleading warning) when <base> is checked out in another worktree.

title: feat(changelog): CHANGELOG.md вҶ’ CHANGELOG.jsonl (append-only JSONL) + prune command
body: Owner: 'CHANGELOGлҘј jsonlлЎң л°”кҫёкі  мҳӨлһҳлҗңкұҙ мӮӯм ң лӘ…л №м–ҙлЎң'. The append-only history moved from a freeform CHANGELOG.md to CHANGELOG.jsonl (newest-first JSONL, {ts,title,body}) so entries are machine-parseable and prunable per-entry. New modules/changelog.ts: add(append, body via stdin, ts=today) / list / render(markdown view) / prune(--keep N | --older-than D, deletes old entries) / migrate(md->jsonl, ran: 240 entries lossless). Rewired the CHANGELOG-MISSING gate everywhere it read CHANGELOG.md -> CHANGELOG.jsonl: harness.config.json lint.changelog.file, pr-cycle doc-gate regex+msg, init.ts scaffold, commons single-doc, CLAUDE/README/ARCHITECTURE docs. cli register + help + commands/changelog.md slash. Old CHANGELOG.md removed.

title: chore(ing): swap the ING status marker рҹ”ө вҶ’ рҹ”„ (in-progress glyph)
body:
Owner picked рҹ”„ (in-progress/refresh) over the blue circle as the ING anchor. Swapped the marker everywhere
the ING enforce reads/writes it: the `ing inject` board header + turn-close directive, the `ing stop-check`
detection regex (`/рҹ”ө ING/` вҶ’ `/рҹ”„ ING/`), and the ARCHITECTURE/README/commons docs that describe it. So the
required per-turn line is now `рҹ”„ ING к°ұмӢ : вҖҰ` / `рҹ”„ ING: ліҖлҸҷ м—ҶмқҢ`. The verify-tier рҹ”ө (SUPPORTED-FORMAL badge
in `verify.ts`/templates) is a different semantic and was left untouched.

title: feat(architecture): stop-check вҖ” enforce a рҹҸӣпёҸ ARCHITECTURE report when code/structure changed
body:
Companion to `ing stop-check`. ING is enforced EVERY turn (progress can shift invisibly); design changes are
rarer, so the architecture enforce is CONDITIONAL вҖ” it must not demand a рҹҸӣпёҸ line on read-only turns.

- **`architecture stop-check`** (new Stop hook В· `decision:block`) вҖ” fires only when the working tree has
  uncommitted code/ARCHITECTURE changes (deterministic `git diff` trigger) AND the last response carries no
  `рҹҸӣпёҸ ARCHITECTURE` line; blocks to force the agent to update the design tree in lockstep or affirm
  `рҹҸӣпёҸ ARCHITECTURE: ліҖлҸҷ м—ҶмқҢ`. Clean tree вҶ’ no-op. Catches codeвҶ”ARCHITECTURE drift (commons cycle-docs-pr).
  sidecar-managed repos only; `stop_hook_active` loop-guard.
- turn-close `рҹҸӣпёҸ н„ҙ л§Ҳк°җ кІҢмқҙнҠё` directive rewritten to require one of the two lines when code/structure
  changed; wired into `hooks/hooks.json` Stop + global `~/.claude/settings.json`.

title: fix(pr-cycle): fall back to `main` when the remote HEAD symref is unset
body:
The doc-gate base was `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref origin/HEAD` stripped of `origin/`, with a
`|| "main"` fallback. But repos created by scaffold (not `git clone`) never get a remote HEAD
symref, so the command returns the literal `origin/HEAD` вҶ’ strips to `"HEAD"` вҖ” non-empty, so
the fallback never fired. The gate then diffed `origin/HEAD...HEAD` (a bogus base), saw a tiny
wrong change set, and fired a misleading "л¬ём„ң м—…лҚ°мқҙнҠё н•„мҲҳ вҖ” CHANGELOG/ARCHITECTURE/README
лҲ„лқҪ" even when those docs WERE updated.

- **modules/pr-cycle.ts** вҖ” treat a stripped result of `"HEAD"` (or empty) as "no default
  known" and fall back to `main`; a real non-main default branch still passes through.
- Verified the branch logic for `HEAD`/empty/`main`/`develop` inputs. (Sibling scaffold repos вҖ”
  `carbon-capture`, `lumen`, `rtsc`, `echoes` вҖ” all lacked the symref; `git remote set-head
  origin main` fixes a repo, and this hardens the tool so an unset symref no longer misleads.)
  (Typecheck CI `npx tsc` is unavailable in this environment; change is type-trivial,
  `node --check` parses clean.)

title: fix(paths): a `.git` dir is a hard boundary for REPO_ROOT discovery
body:
`findRepoRoot()` walked the whole ancestry for a `harness.config.json` BEFORE falling back to the
nearest `.git`, so a config in a non-git parent could hijack REPO_ROOT and break every git call.

Hit in `dancinlab/`: sibling repos (`carbon-capture`, `lumen`, `rtsc`, `echoes`) each have their own
`.git` but no per-repo `harness.config.json`, while the parent monorepo `dancinlab/harness.config.json`
exists and is NOT a git repo. From any sibling, `REPO_ROOT` resolved to `/Users/.../dancinlab`, so
`pr-cycle` ran git there вҶ’ `fatal: not a git repository`, mis-counted the change set, and the doc gate
fired spuriously. (`ing` was unaffected вҖ” it resolves git independently.)

- **lib/paths.ts** вҖ” `findRepoRoot()` now returns the current dir as soon as it has EITHER a
  `harness.config.json` (checked first, so an in-dir config still wins) OR a `.git` directory; it never
  ascends past a git root. Dropped the `firstGit` deferral. Discovery-order comment updated.
- Verified: from `carbon-capture` and `lumen`, `REPO_ROOT` now resolves to each repo's own root (was the
  non-git parent); `~/.sidecar/cli` and the monorepo root itself are unchanged; a deep subdir climbs to its
  `.git` boundary. End-to-end, `pr-cycle` from a sibling repo now reaches its real branch/doc logic.
  (Typecheck CI `npx tsc` is unavailable in this environment вҖ” no global/local typescript; change is
  type-trivial and `node --check` parses clean.)

title: feat(ing): per-turn ING-status enforce вҖ” every response must carry a рҹ”ө ING line (Stop gate)
body:
The turn-close ING directive was advisory ("update the board if progress changed, else skip"), and the only
hard signal was a code-edit counter (`ing staleness-check`, warn-only). But progress changes WITHOUT a code
edit too вҖ” a measurement, a verdict, a bench result, a background-agent landing вҖ” so an edit-counter misses
those turns. Owner asked to enforce an ING update every turn.

- **`ing stop-check`** (new Stop hook В· `decision:block`) вҖ” mirrors `recommend stop-check`: reads the last
  assistant text and blocks the turn if it carries no `рҹ”ө ING` line, forcing the model to emit one. NOT tied
  to file edits (captures measurement/verdict/agent-landing progress); the agent consciously reports either
  `рҹ”ө ING к°ұмӢ : <what>` (board mutated) or `рҹ”ө ING: ліҖлҸҷ м—ҶмқҢ` (no-change). Scoped to sidecar-managed repos
  (harness.config.json); native `stop_hook_active` loop-guard caps it at once per chain.
- **turn-close directive** (`ing inject`) rewritten: every response must include one of the two `рҹ”ө ING`
  lines; progress explicitly includes non-code signals.
- Wired into the plugin `hooks/hooks.json` Stop array and the global `~/.claude/settings.json`.
- `ing staleness-check` (code-edit warn) stays as a secondary signal.

title: chore(changelog+styles): retire the user-request quote convention + explicit slug-block severity
body:
The owner asked to drop the speaker-emoji user-request quote line that CHANGELOG entries had been opening
with, and to check the plugin wasn't *forcing* it. Audit: no hook/lint/template hard-mandated it вҖ” the only
soft nudge was the easy response-style listing "commit-message body user-summary section" as a Tier-A scope,
which pulled friendly user-summary prose (and the habitual user-request quote) into commit/CHANGELOG bodies.

- **easy styles** вҖ” removed the "commit-message body user-summary section" scope line from all five
  `styles/easy*.md` (en/ko/ja/zh/ru). Easy prose no longer applies to commit bodies, so nothing nudges a
  user-summary/quote there. (Self-update propagates the global `~/.sidecar/cli/styles/` copies.)
- **CHANGELOG** вҖ” scrubbed all 51 historical user-request quote lines; the convention is retired, not
  authored going forward.
- **severity-map** вҖ” mapped `ARCH-SLUG-MISSING` / `ARCH-SLUG-DUPE` / `ARCH-SLUG-FORMAT` to explicit `block`
  (was fallback-block only), so a slug-less / dup / malformed-slug tree node is a first-class hard block at
  commit time (git hook + global settings.json commit-lint gate). Verified: missing slug вҶ’ `[block]`, exit 1.

title: feat(lab): scaffolded CLAUDE.md documents the hexa-lang stdlib + QFORGE conventions
body:
`/lab init`'s generated `CLAUDE.md` now carries two demiurge cross-repo conventions up front,
so every new lab repo states them from day one (lifted from `demiurge/CLAUDE.md` d3 / d_qforge_*):

- **hexa-lang stdlib** (demiurge d3) вҖ” implementation code lives in the canonical `hexa-lang`
  stdlib; this repo *consumes* it and never owns stdlib. Topical/domain folders hold docs /
  manifests only вҖ” no duplicated implementation, not a code home. Reusable logic вҶ’ upstream.
- **QFORGE compute default** (demiurge d_qforge_default) вҖ” heavy compute (DFT/DFPT/el-ph/sim)
  defaults to the from-scratch `QFORGE` stack, not QE/bespoke; QE is reference/fallback for
  un-migrated pieces only, migrate piece-by-piece behind a вүӨ1 % gate, decks via `hexa deck`.
  Marked *(skip for non-compute labs)*.
- `tool/` gotcha clarified: thin verification harness only, not a home for reusable impl.

title: feat(lab): `/lab init` вҖ” scaffold the lumen/rtsc/carbon-capture sibling-repo skeleton
body:
The lumen / rtsc / carbon-capture sibling repos all share one skeleton (src/ В· state/ В· ARCHITECTURE.json +
architecture.html viewer + serve.py В· CLAUDE/README/CHANGELOG В· HYPOTHESES/ pre-registerвҶ’falsifyвҶ’runвҶ’verdict В·
tool/<slug>.py shared harness). It was hand-rebuilt each time. `/lab init` mints it in one shot.

- **`lab init [dir] [--force] [--dry-run] [--name N] [--desc D]`** (`modules/lab.ts` вҶ’ `runLab`, dispatched
  in `cli/index.ts`) вҖ” recursively renders the tokenized skeleton in `templates/lab/` (`{{NAME}}` /
  `{{SLUG_SNAKE}}` / `{{DESC}}` / `{{DATE}}`, applied to file CONTENTS and the `tool/{{SLUG_SNAKE}}.py` path)
  into a target repo. Never overwrites an existing file unless `--force`; `--dry-run` reports only. Name
  auto-detects from `basename(dir)` (override with `--name`); default target = cwd. Files-only вҖ” no git
  init/push (outward-facing git stays out of scope, matching `init`). Empty dirs kept via `.gitkeep`.
- **`commands/lab.md`** delegator (shadowed to bare `/lab`); ARCHITECTURE.json + TOOLKIT.jsonl entries added.
- Verified: dry-run lists 15 files writes nothing В· real scaffold renders `tool/<slug>.py` В· valid JSON В·
  serve.py parses В· harness imports В· no unrendered tokens В· non-clobber + `--force` both correct.

title: feat(pre): global commit-lint gate вҖ” enforce the lint gate via settings.json, not a per-repo git hook
body:
The lint commit-gate lived ONLY in a per-repo `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (installed by `sidecar init`), so a
repo that was never init-ed (e.g. anima) committed completely un-gated вҖ” its 89 oversized ARCHITECTURE
cells never blocked a commit. Since `sidecar pre bash` is already wired GLOBALLY via `~/.claude/settings.json`
PreToolUse(Bash), an agent `git commit` can be intercepted there to run the SAME lint everywhere.

- **`pre.ts` commit interceptor** (`COMMIT-LINT`) вҖ” detects an agent `git commit`, and in a sidecar-managed
  repo (harness.config.json present) runs the lint gate, blocking on any block-severity violation with the
  list + fix hint. `git commit -a/-am/--all` widens the lint scope to the auto-staged tracked-modified set
  (precise `-a` detection, no over-match вҶ’ no false block). Honors the same `--no-verify`/`# no-verify-ok`
  escape the danger guard governs (one consistent opt-out, not a new hatch).
- **`lint.ts` refactor** вҖ” extracted `collectViolations()` (pure, no printing) + `lintBlockers()` (block-severity
  filter) from `runLint`, exported `Violation`. The git pre-commit hook and the global interceptor now share
  ONE lint implementation (native-canonical-first, no reinvent).
- Net: the enforce gate is now global вҖ” every sidecar-managed repo (anima included) gets agent-commit lint
  enforcement from the shared settings.json, no per-repo `sidecar init --hooks` needed. The per-repo git hook
  still covers non-agent (human/script) commits.

title: feat(architecture): retire кө¬л¶„/type field вҶ’ searchable per-node slug + `architecture search`
body:
The ARCHITECTURE.json tree nodes carried a `кө¬л¶„` (type/category) column that wasn't grep-friendly and
the owner found dispensable. Replaced it with a stable, searchable `slug` per node + a search verb:
- **Schema migration** вҖ” every tree node (126) now has a unique kebab-case `slug`; the `кө¬л¶„` field is
  dropped and its category folded into the slug prefix (`force-push к°Җл“ң` вҶ’ `guard-force-push`, `ing` вҶ’
  `module-ing`, вҖҰ) so zero category info is lost and `search guard`/`search module`/`search config` work.
  `columns` кө¬л¶„вҶ’slug. The HTML viewer is column-driven, so it renders + searches the slug column with
  no viewer change.
- **`sidecar architecture search <query>`** вҖ” case-insensitive substring over slug / мқҙлҰ„ / м—ӯн•  / мғҒм„ё,
  prints each matching node's slug + breadcrumb path. Korean queries still match via мқҙлҰ„/мғҒм„ё text.
- **slug lint** вҖ” `lintArchitectureTree` now emits `ARCH-SLUG-MISSING` / `ARCH-SLUG-DUPE` /
  `ARCH-SLUG-FORMAT` (block via severity-map fallback), so every node stays addressable and slugs stay
  unique kebab-case. `init.ts` scaffolds the slug schema for new repos.

title: feat(commons): retire the poll discipline вҖ” drop poll-min-30m/poll-max-10m rules, both guards, the /poll runbook
body:
a flapping host, capping check frequency), so the owner asked to remove it entirely.

Removed the whole poll-discipline subsystem (was c19 `poll-min-30m` + c21/c22 `poll-max-10m`):
- **commons rules dropped** вҖ” `poll-min-30m` (poll external long-runners вүҘ30min) and `poll-max-10m`
  (check live long-runners вүҘ10min) deleted from `config/commons.md`.
- **both enforcement guards deleted** вҖ” `modules/poll-guard.ts` (POLL-INTERVAL block on short bash poll
  loops) and `modules/heartbeat-guard.ts` (POLL-HEARTBEAT stale-long-runner warn). Unwired from
  `pre.ts` (POLL-INTERVAL), `post.ts` (heartbeat stamp/warn + bg-launch auto-track), `ing.ts` (live
  marker + heartbeat warn вҖ” `ing pod add/rm` bookkeeping kept), and `lib/config.ts` (`poll.maxSilenceSec`).
- **/poll runbook retired** вҖ” `commands/poll.md`, `templates/poll.md`, `runbooks.ts::runPoll`, the
  `cli/index.ts` import/help/dispatch, and the ARCHITECTURE poll-guard/heartbeat/runbook cells +
  the two convergence records (`NO_SHORT_POLL_LOOP`, `NO_ABANDONED_LONGRUNNER`). README + marketplace
  guard lists and the ci-track `c19` parentheticals scrubbed of the now-dead rule ids.
- Lean-trimmed the pre-existing over-cap `ci` ARCHITECTURE cell (743вҶ’<300 chars В· surfaced by the
  ARCH-BIG-CELL lint while shipping this) to keep the design tree green.

`ci-track --watch` (remote PR/CI polling) is a CLI feature, not the poll discipline вҖ” it stays.

title: feat(ci): scaffold cost-free fast CI path вҖ” self-hosted pool в–ё github-hosted fallback + warm cache (no Blacksmith)
body:
`sidecar ci scaffold` к°Җ л§Ңл“ңлҠ” `.github/workflows/ci.yml` мқ„ fast-free кө¬мЎ°лЎң м—…к·ёл Ҳмқҙл“ң.
кё°мЎҙм—” лӢЁмқј мһЎ `runs-on: ${ci.runner}`(кё°ліё ubuntu-latest)лҝҗ вҖ” pool мҡ°м„ В·fallbackВ·мәҗмӢң м—ҶмқҢ.
мқҙм ң config `ci.fallback`/`ci.cachePaths`(л‘ҳ лӢӨ optionalВ·лҜём„Өм •=кё°мЎҙ лӢЁмқјмһЎ к·ёлҢҖлЎң=н•ҳмң„нҳёнҷҳ):

- **`ci.fallback`** м„Өм • мӢң вҶ’ `pick-runner` dispatch мһЎ(л¬ҙлЈҢ github-hostedВ·timeout 2л¶„)мқҙ
  `gh api .../actions/runners` лЎң self-hosted pool мқҳ ONLINE+idle лҹ¬л„ҲлҘј probe вҶ’
  мһҲмңјл©ҙ `ci.runner`(self-hostedВ·public repo л¬ҙлЈҢ 12-core), м—Ҷмңјл©ҙ `ci.fallback`(л¬ҙлЈҢ github-hosted).
  `verify` мһЎмқҖ `runs-on: ${{ fromJSON(needs.pick-runner.outputs.runs_on) }}`.
  **safe-by-construction**: probe м—җлҹ¬(PAT л¶Җмһ¬ 403В·offline) вҶ’ fallback мқҙлқј CI к°Җ мҳҒкө¬ нҒҗлҢҖкё° м•Ҳ н•Ё.
  pool м„ нҳёлҘј мӢӨм ң мјңл Өл©ҙ repo secret `RUNNER_PROBE_TOKEN`(admin:read PAT) вҖ” м—Ҷмңјл©ҙ м•Ҳм „н•ҳкІҢ л¬ҙлЈҢ fallback.
- **`ci.cachePaths`** м„Өм • мӢң вҶ’ checkout л’Ө `actions/cache@v4` мҠӨн…қ(`key=os-proj-ci-<sha>` +
  `restore-keys` warm мһ¬мӮ¬мҡ©). ccache/нҲҙмІҙмқё/deps л”°лң»н•ҳкІҢ мһ¬мӮ¬мҡ© = л°ҳліө л№Ңл“ң к°ҖмҶҚ.
- `runnerToJson()` н—¬нҚј: `[self-hosted, Linux, X64]` YAML лҰ¬мҠӨнҠё вҶ” JSON л°°м—ҙ вҶ” лӢЁмқј лқјлІЁ л¬ёмһҗм—ҙмқ„
  `fromJSON()` н•ң м¶ңл ҘмңјлЎң нҶөн•©. `serializeStep` мқҖ л©ҖнӢ°лқјмқё `with` к°’(мҳҲ: cache `path`)мқ„ YAML лҰ¬н„°лҹҙ лё”лЎқ(`|`)мңјлЎң emit.

к·јкұ°: GitHub larger runner лҠ” public repo лҸ„ per-minute кіјкёҲ(Blacksmith мҷҖ лҸҷмқј н•Ём •), н‘ңмӨҖ/self-hosted лҹ¬л„Ҳл§Ң
public repo л¬ҙлЈҢ(GitHub docs). л”°лқјм„ң "Blacksmith м—Ҷмқҙ мөңлҢҖн•ң л№ лҘё CI" = self-hosted pool мҡ°м„  + л¬ҙлЈҢ fallback + мәҗмӢң.
м„Өкі„ кІ°м •мқ„ мқјнҡҢм„ұ repo к°Җ м•„лӢҲлқј **ci н”Ңлҹ¬к·ёмқё н‘ңмӨҖ**мңјлЎң л°•м•„ м „ repo м „нҢҢ(root-cause).

кІҖмҰқ: `ciWorkflowYaml` л‘җ ліҖмў…(no-fallback н•ҳмң„нҳёнҷҳ / fallback+cache) л ҢлҚ” вҶ’ python `yaml.safe_load` м–‘мӘҪ OK В·
`runnerToJson` 3 мјҖмқҙмҠӨ вң“ В· `cli help` rc=0 лЎңл“ң.
